name: Gladly
one_liner: Gladly is a customer service/helpdesk platform centered on people (customers) rather than tickets, with a REST API for customers, conversations, communications, tasks, and more.
primary_auth: Token-based HTTP Basic Authentication: agent email as username, API token as password, passed in the Authorization header over HTTPS.
access_notes: API tokens are self-serve within the Gladly UI (Settings > App Developer Tools) but only for users granted the 'API User' role inside an existing Gladly instance. Gladly is an enterprise SaaS with no public free/dev signup or open sandbox, so obtaining working credentials requires being a paying Gladly customer. No human approval beyond internal role assignment.
api_notes: Documented REST API at developer.gladly.com/rest covers Agents, Customers, Conversations, Communications (SMS/email), Tasks, Teams & Inboxes, Topics, Answers, Business Hours, Reports, Events, Webhooks, and Export. Roughly a few dozen operations across these resources.
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped Gladly MCP server found. Searches surfaced only speculative articles and unrelated MCP servers (Glean, Gladia); no official or notable community Gladly MCP server located.
main_blocker: No public free/trial/sandbox path to credentials; you must be an existing paying Gladly customer with a user assigned the API User role to generate tokens.

evidence.claim: Gladly REST API covers Customers, Conversations, Communications, Tasks, Webhooks, Export and more, using token-based Basic Authentication over HTTPS.
API tokens are created self-serve in Settings > App Developer Tools by a user with the API User role.
Gladly provides REST API setup/testing tutorials and Node.js examples for developers.

open_questions: Whether every Gladly subscription tier includes API access, and exact total operation count (breadth could reach the higher end of MODERATE). No confirmed pricing detail on API availability.
